description: Targeted attacks on specific users, repeated callouts, coordinated pile-ons

scenarios.description: Repeated targeting of the same user across multiple posts
scenarios.postText: Everyone stay away from @user123 — they're a known liar and a fraud and have been scamming people for years
scenarios.expectedReasoning: pattern of targeting the same user combined with public callout

scenarios.description: Mutual heated exchange — both parties engaged, not one-sided
scenarios.postText: You have no idea what you're talking about, stop commenting on things you don't understand
scenarios.expectedReasoning: heated but mutual exchange, no specific targeting pattern, single comment

scenarios.description: Pile-on in comments coordinated against a specific user
scenarios.postText: Can we all agree @userXYZ needs to be removed from this community? Who else has had problems with them?
scenarios.expectedReasoning: explicit call for coordinated action against a named user

scenarios.description: Apparent harassment that reveals itself as retaliatory reporting — demonstrates multi-step agentic reasoning
scenarios.postText: I disagree with @user456 on basically everything they post, their takes are consistently wrong
scenarios.expectedReasoning: reporter has filed 40+ reports targeting the same user — report is retaliatory, post itself is critical opinion not harassment
scenarios.agenticNote: This scenario is designed to show non-linear tool use. The agent starts investigating the post author (get_user_history, get_user_violations), finds nothing notable, then checks get_reporter_history and discovers the reporter is the bad actor. The final decision is driven by a signal the agent found only because an earlier tool result prompted a new question — the tool sequence is emergent, not predetermined.
